critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — SELECTION BIAS FROM APPROVED-ONLY TRAINING: Model conditioned on human underwriting approval. Cannot assess performance for rejected applicants. Standard deployment against full applicant pool would introduce biased risk estimates.

C2 — REGULATORY UNCERTAINTY ON ALTERNATIVE DATA: FCRA obligations differ for rent and utility payment histories vs bureau tradelines.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — The second-stage filter deployment architecture directly addresses this. Model only scores applicants who have already passed human underwriting using the same criteria as training. Selection condition is reproduced at inference time. The bias concern does not apply within this deployment design.

C2 — Addressable compliance matter. Not a basis for model rejection.
adjudication_raw: One round resolves both points. The survivorship bias concern is operationally mitigated by the documented second-stage constraint. The regulatory concern is a compliance process matter. Defense wins.
